description_long: This is a traditional Japanese street food stall, constructed primarily from dark, weathered wood with a pitched roof and a red fabric banner adorned with white Japanese characters. The stall is mounted on a sturdy wooden cart with large spoked wheels and features a long wooden bench for customers. The front of the stall has a serving counter with various cooking utensils and containers visible, while the side has an extended wooden table for additional workspace or customer use. The roof is supported by wooden beams and is partially open on the sides, providing shelter while allowing ventilation. The overall design is compact and functional, evoking a nostalgic, rustic charm typical of classic ramen or yatai stalls found in Japanese urban settings.
description: A traditional Japanese wooden street food stall with a red banner, pitched roof, serving counter, and attached bench, evoking a nostalgic, rustic charm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red banner with Japanese text, serving counter, and attached bench.
description_view_1: Top-rear angled view highlighting the pitched roof, extended side table, and bench alignment.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the large spoked wheel, side table, and open serving area with visible utensils.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden back panel, roof structure, and partial bench visibility.
attribute_signage: Red fabric banner with white Japanese characters, placed at the front upper edge of the stall, traditional style.
attribute_serving_counter: A wide, rectangular wooden counter at the front of the stall, made of dark wood, with space for serving and displaying food items.
attribute_food_display: Food is displayed in open containers and pots on the counter, easily visible and accessible to customers, with no protective glass or cover.
